# rex

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 245
- **Lines of code**: 86,113
- **Languages**: TypeScript (227), JSON (9), Other (4), Markdown (4), Text (1)
- **Zones**: 13 (13 with descriptions)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-08T06:04:26.536Z

## Architecture Zones

### CLI End-to-End Tests (`cli-e2e-tests`)

Contains the full suite of end-to-end CLI tests covering adapter, analyze, import, prune, quiet mode, recommend, smart-add, sync, and other command paths.

- Files: 13
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cli-e2e-tests/context.md`

### Data Persistence & Validation (`data-persistence-validation`)

The storage and integrity layer: file-based PRD adapter, schema validation, and the report command that surfaces store state.

- Files: 8
- Cohesion: 0.17, Coupling: 0.83
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/report.ts, src/schema/validate.ts, src/store/file-adapter.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/data-persistence-validation/context.md`

### Integration Schemas (`integration-schemas`)

Defines and exports the Zod/type schemas that describe the data shapes exchanged with external integrations (Jira, Notion) at the store boundary.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 0.31, Coupling: 0.69
- Entry points: src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts, src/store/integration-schemas/jira.ts, src/store/integration-schemas/notion.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/integration-schemas/context.md`

### Mutation Commands (`mutation-commands`)

CLI commands and supporting infrastructure for PRD mutation operations: move, remove, update, verify, adapter management, and the output/error formatting layer.

- Files: 25
- Cohesion: 0.33, Coupling: 0.67
- Entry points: src/cli/errors.ts, src/cli/output.ts, src/cli/validate-input.ts, src/core/parent-completion.ts, src/core/parent-reset.ts (+1 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/mutation-commands/context.md`

### Notion Integration Adapter (`notion-integration`)

Implements the Notion sync adapter including data mapping and store-level adapter logic for syncing PRD items with Notion databases.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.14, Coupling: 0.86
- Entry points: src/store/notion-adapter.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/notion-integration/context.md`

### PRD Analysis Pipeline (`prd-analysis-pipeline`)

The full PRD analysis subsystem: LLM-driven proposal generation, deduplication, consolidation, decomposition, extraction, guided-analysis flows, and the CLI commands that orchestrate them.

- Files: 73
- Cohesion: 0.63, Coupling: 0.37
- Entry points: src/analyze/consolidation-guard.ts, src/analyze/decompose.ts, src/analyze/dedupe.ts, src/analyze/extract.ts, src/analyze/file-validation.ts (+15 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-analysis-pipeline/context.md`

### PRD Fix Command (`prd-fix-command`)

The isolated fix operation: a CLI command, its core implementation, and co-located unit tests.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.25, Coupling: 0.75
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/fix.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-fix-command/context.md`

### PRD Operations Core (`prd-operations-core`)

The central business logic hub for all PRD mutation, lifecycle, and schema operations across the rex package.

- Files: 43
- Cohesion: 0.44, Coupling: 0.56
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/add.ts, src/cli/commands/reorganize.ts, src/core/cascade-reset.ts, src/core/delete.ts, src/core/facets.ts (+17 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-operations-core/context.md`

### Project Root (`project-root`)

Top-level project scaffolding: package manifest, TypeScript config, test runner config, and AI-readable context artifacts generated by sourcevision.

- Files: 9
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/project-root/context.md`

### Remote Integration (`remote-integration`)

Cross-cutting utilities and remote-sync infrastructure: PRD tree/DAG primitives, recommendation creation, remote adapter registry, and the Notion integration client.

- Files: 30
- Cohesion: 0.25, Coupling: 0.75
- Entry points: src/analyze/acknowledge.ts, src/cli/commands/recommend.ts, src/cli/commands/sync.ts, src/core/canonical.ts, src/core/dag.ts (+7 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/remote-integration/context.md`

### Rex Runtime Data (`rex-runtime-data`)

Persisted runtime state for the rex PRD tracker: project config, the PRD tree, execution log, and human-readable workflow state.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-runtime-data/context.md`

### Rex Status & MCP CLI (`rex-status-mcp-cli`)

The read-oriented CLI surface for rex: status display, health checks, usage analytics, and MCP server wiring.

- Files: 20
- Cohesion: 0.33, Coupling: 0.67
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/constants.ts, src/cli/commands/health.ts, src/cli/commands/init.ts, src/cli/commands/status.ts, src/cli/commands/token-format.ts (+4 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-status-mcp-cli/context.md`

### Task Selection Engine (`task-selection-engine`)

The next-task selection logic: scoring, filtering, prioritization, and the CLI command that surfaces it.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 0.17, Coupling: 0.83
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/next.ts, src/core/next-task.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/task-selection-engine/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/core/tree.ts` (49 imports)
- `src/schema/index.ts` (44 imports)
- `src/store/index.ts` (31 imports)
- `src/cli/commands/constants.ts` (29 imports)
- `src/cli/output.ts` (28 imports)
- `src/cli/errors.ts` (26 imports)
- `src/analyze/reason.ts` (23 imports)
- `src/core/canonical.ts` (16 imports)
- `src/store/project-config.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyze/dedupe.ts` (8 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 116 files)
- `@n-dx/llm-client` (used by 13 files)
- `zod` (used by 7 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `ora` (used by 1 files)

### Circular Dependencies

Found 1 circular dependency chain(s):

- `src/cli/commands/status-sections.ts` → `src/cli/commands/status.ts`

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- store/index.ts violates the project's explicit gateway rule ('gateways re-export; they contain no logic') by implementing 5 factory functions (createStore, createStoreWithConfig, createNotionStore, resolveStore, resolveRemoteStore) inline. Factory logic should be extracted to src/store/factory.ts with store/index.ts becoming a pure re-export barrel as specified in CLAUDE.md. [warning] (data-persistence-validation)
- The index.ts barrel re-exports both jira.ts and notion.ts schemas but notion-integration (the natural consumer of notion.ts) has no import link to this barrel — the barrel's primary consumer for Notion schema types is some zone other than the adapter that interprets those schemas, inverting the expected schema→adapter data-flow [warning] (integration-schemas)
- cli/help.ts is misclassified as [types] despite containing runtime formatting logic and an @n-dx/llm-client dependency. Archetype should be [utility] or [service] — the wrong classification distorts coupling metrics for this zone and may suppress findings about the cross-package dependency. [info] (mutation-commands)
- src/cli/index.ts (the CLI entry point) is zone-assigned to mutation-commands rather than to a dedicated entrypoint or orchestration zone. This couples the neutral entry point to the mutation-command zone boundary, making future extraction of mutation commands into a sub-package harder. [warning] (mutation-commands)
- notion-map.ts has no independent zone-level consumers — it is only reachable through notion-adapter.ts within the same zone, making it effectively private implementation of the adapter; if it has no standalone test coverage or external consumers, it should be merged into notion-adapter.ts rather than maintained as a separate file [warning] (notion-integration)
- src/cli/commands/analyze.ts and src/cli/commands/smart-add.ts import atomic-write directly from the store layer internals rather than delegating file persistence to a store service or facade. CLI commands should not import store primitives directly. [warning] (prd-analysis-pipeline)
- SyncEngine is defined in src/core/sync-engine.ts (core layer) but re-exported from src/store/index.ts (store layer). Consumers importing SyncEngine via store/index.ts form a dependency on the store layer when they actually need a core concept, and the store gateway now implicitly exposes a core internal. SyncEngine should either live in the store layer or be exported directly from a core barrel. [warning] (prd-operations-core)
- src/core/ contains 32 modules with no index.ts — all 32 internal filenames are implicit public API. Any consumer (mcp-tools.ts, CLI commands, hench gateway) directly path-imports internal modules. There is no mechanism to distinguish public core operations from private implementation helpers, making the core zone's stability contract invisible and unenforceable. [warning] (prd-operations-core)
- acknowledge.ts implements its own file I/O (readFile/writeFile) in parallel with atomic-write.ts and file-adapter.ts — two independent persistence implementations exist with no shared abstraction, meaning concurrency and error-handling improvements must be applied in multiple places. [warning] (remote-integration)
- src/analyze/acknowledge.ts is a store-layer module (owns acknowledged-findings.json, exports AcknowledgedStore/AcknowledgedFinding, performs atomic reads/writes) co-located in the analyze/ directory and zone-assigned to remote-integration solely because recommend.ts imports it. It belongs in src/store/ alongside file-adapter.ts. [warning] (remote-integration)
- ... and 4 more

### Patterns

- cli-e2e-tests achieves coupling 0 despite covering adapter-related CLI commands (cli-adapter.test.ts, cli-sync.test.ts) — the integration layer is fully encapsulated behind CLI boundaries, which is the correct abstraction and should be preserved as the integration surface grows [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- The index.ts barrel in integration-schemas re-exports both Jira and Notion schemas, but the Notion adapter lives in a separate zone with no import from this barrel — the barrel does not serve its natural consumer, undermining its purpose as a unified schema surface [warning] (integration-schemas)
- notion-map.ts (utility archetype) lives in a zone whose entry point is notion-adapter.ts (store archetype) — the utility has a higher-layer host, inverting the conventional utility-below-consumer layering [warning] (notion-integration)
- The 20 reverse imports from core into unit-analyze break the otherwise clean directional boundary; if any of these pull in analysis-pipeline-specific types or utilities, they represent an upward dependency that must be extracted into a shared schema or moved to the call site. [warning] (prd-analysis-pipeline)
- Duplicate generated output locations (root context.md vs .sourcevision/CONTEXT.md) create a two-source-of-truth problem; tooling that reads the wrong copy will silently operate on stale data. [warning] (project-root)
- Extracting dag.ts, tree.ts, and canonical.ts into the core zone and isolating notion-client and adapter-registry into a dedicated integrations/ zone would dissolve this artificial hub and restore remote-integration to its intended role as a leaf. [warning] (remote-integration)
- A Zod or JSON Schema validator applied at the single read-site (store load) would convert the invisible runtime coupling into an explicit, type-safe contract and catch schema drift at startup rather than mid-operation. [warning] (rex-runtime-data)
- src/cli/commands/constants.ts is a shared-constants file accidentally parked inside a high-level CLI/MCP zone; its 7-zone import fan-out means it is effectively a foundation-layer file masquerading as a CLI file — move it to src/schema/ or src/core/ to reflect its actual scope [warning] (rex-status-mcp-cli)
- task-selection-engine → prd-operations-core is a clean unidirectional dependency (9 imports, 0 return): this zone correctly acts as a pure consumer of the domain hub with no upward coupling, representing the healthiest structural pattern in the package [info] (task-selection-engine)

### Relationships

- cli-init.test.ts belongs semantically and structurally in the e2e zone (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0) — its presence here dilutes e2e zone purity and inflates data-persistence-validation coupling; this is a misplacement, not a design decision [warning] (data-persistence-validation)
- integration-schemas (pure schema types) is consumed by remote-integration (3 imports) but has no import link to notion-integration despite both serving the same external service — schema and adapter are decoupled from each other while both couple to separate internal zones, a leaky abstraction at the service boundary [warning] (integration-schemas)
- 14 imports from unit-analyze into unit-cli (mutation-commands consuming analysis pipeline internals) create a layering violation: the command layer should call high-level service functions, not import from the analysis pipeline directly. [warning] (mutation-commands)
- The 22 mutual imports between mutation-commands and the cli zone indicate they are effectively one zone with an arbitrary split boundary; the high bidirectional traffic should trigger a deliberate merge-or-separate decision rather than continued organic growth. [warning] (mutation-commands)
- notion-integration (adapter+map) and integration-schemas (schema) anchor to different internal zones — prd-operations-core and remote-integration respectively — so the Notion integration contract has two separate internal coupling points with no shared interface zone to unify them [warning] (notion-integration)
- prd-analysis-pipeline → core is the healthiest cross-zone relationship in the codebase: high volume (49 imports), predominantly unidirectional, and cohesion-preserving. Use it as the benchmark when evaluating other zone pairs. [info] (prd-analysis-pipeline)
- prd-fix-command and mutation-commands are both leaf command zones that should have no peer dependency on each other; the 1+1 bidirectional coupling is most likely a shared-constants artifact — confirm and remove to achieve clean fan-in-only topology for both zones [warning] (prd-fix-command)
- prd-operations-core imports constants.ts from rex-status-mcp-cli (a higher-layer zone), creating an upward dependency that inverts the intended domain→CLI layering; constants.ts should be extracted to a shared foundation layer accessible to both zones without hierarchy violation [critical] (prd-operations-core)
- remote-integration acts as a second gravity well alongside core — it has bidirectional coupling with every major production zone, making it impossible to evolve the remote sync subsystem without touching the analysis pipeline, mutation commands, and MCP layer simultaneously. [critical] (remote-integration)
- Runtime data files are the only cross-layer coupling mechanism not visible in the import graph — every production zone that reads prd.json or config.json has an invisible dependency on this zone that bypasses all architectural boundary checks. [warning] (rex-runtime-data)
- ... and 2 more

### Observations

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- Cohesion 1.0 and coupling 0 confirm the e2e suite is architecturally isolated — an excellent baseline that should be preserved as new commands are added. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- The breadth of CLI commands covered (adapter, analyze, import, prune, quiet, recommend, smart-add, sync plus 5 more) indicates strong end-to-end coverage; cross-reference with the CLI command list to identify any untested commands. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- This zone is the only one with perfect cohesion in the entire codebase — a sign that the e2e layer has well-maintained boundaries compared to production zones. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- High coupling (0.83) — 12 imports target "prd-operations-core" [warning] (data-persistence-validation)
- Low cohesion (0.17) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (data-persistence-validation)
- Cohesion 0.17 and coupling 0.83 are flag-level metrics, but inspection of the three production files (file-adapter.ts, validate.ts, report.ts) shows they form a coherent persistence/integrity triad; the metrics are dominated by test cross-imports. [warning] (data-persistence-validation)
- cli-init.test.ts (an e2e test) is grouped in this zone alongside unit tests — confirm it belongs here semantically or whether it should migrate to the e2e zone (which already has cohesion 1.0). [info] (data-persistence-validation)
- store-contract.test.ts validates the contract boundary between store/contracts.ts (in core) and file-adapter.ts; this test is high-value for catching regressions when the store interface evolves. [info] (data-persistence-validation)
- 1 circular dependency chain detected — see imports.json for details [warning]
- ... and 55 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "data-persistence-validation" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (data-persistence-validation)
- report.ts is a CLI display command ([cli-command] archetype) whose natural peer group is status.ts, usage.ts, and health.ts in rex-status-mcp-cli, not the persistence/validation files in this zone — move report.ts to the display zone and rename data-persistence-validation to 'persistence-validation' to accurately reflect the remaining files' shared responsibility [warning] (data-persistence-validation)
- Zone "Data Persistence & Validation" (data-persistence-validation) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.83, cohesion: 0.17, coupling: 0.83) — requires immediate architectural intervention [critical] (data-persistence-validation)
- src/cli/commands/ contains files from 8 zones (data-persistence-validation, mutation-commands, prd-analysis-pipeline, prd-fix-command, prd-operations-core, remote-integration, rex-status-mcp-cli, task-selection-engine) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Five files named identically across two directory levels exist in the package (fix.ts×2, health.ts×2, next/next-task asymmetry, stats.ts×?) — adopt a naming convention where core domain modules use a noun describing the domain concept (tree, stats, health-score) and CLI command wrappers use an imperative verb (fix-cmd.ts, report.ts, next.ts). This eliminates ambiguity in stack traces and grep output without changing public APIs. [info]
- Introduce a tests/support/ directory for shared test fixtures and utilities. The unit-analyze → unit-cli (14) cross-test-zone dependency indicates that test helpers are currently hosted in a test zone they do not conceptually belong to. A shared support zone with zero production coupling would absorb these helpers and reduce cross-test-zone coupling to near zero. [warning]
- Introduce an explicit archetype-to-directory mapping for src/cli/: entry-point commands in src/cli/commands/, shared display helpers in src/cli/renderers/ (token-format.ts, status-sections.ts), and MCP transport files in src/cli/mcp/ (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts). This three-subdirectory split maps directly onto the three concerns currently blended in rex-status-mcp-cli and would raise cohesion by making file placement self-documenting. [warning]
- No production zone achieves both high cohesion (>0.5) and low coupling (<0.3) simultaneously. The minimum intervention to create the first such zone is to isolate the task-selection-engine zone: it already has 5 dedicated test files and a unidirectional import profile (9 outbound to core, 1 inbound). Introducing a gateway or index.ts barrel for task-selection-engine would formalize this boundary and produce the package's first provably low-coupling production zone. [info]
- The 'core module required' decision has no documented criterion — currently applied to fix (1 consumer), next-task (2+ consumers), health (3 consumers) without a visible rule. Document in PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md: 'A src/core/X.ts module is warranted when (a) two or more consumers exist, or (b) the logic is independently unit-tested with 3+ test cases.' This makes single-consumer core modules (fix.ts) visible as candidates for consolidation. [info]
- The 'fix' zone (4 files) has no corresponding src/fix/ directory, making it undiscoverable by directory navigation. Identify the 4 member files, redistribute them to their semantically correct directories (core/, cli/commands/, etc.), and dissolve the zone. A zone with no physical directory home is an architectural liability, not an asset. [warning]
- ... and 39 more

## File Inventory

| Path | Role | Archetype | Zone |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| `.claude/settings.local.json` | other |  |  |
| `.rex/config.json` | other |  | rex-runtime-data |
| `.rex/execution-log.jsonl` | other |  | rex-runtime-data |
| `.rex/prd.json` | other |  | rex-runtime-data |
| `.rex/workflow.md` | docs |  | rex-runtime-data |
| `README.md` | docs |  | project-root |
| `Rex-F.png` | asset |  | project-root |
| `Rex.png` | asset |  | project-root |
| `context.md` | docs |  | project-root |
| `llms.txt` | docs |  | project-root |
| `package-lock.json` | generated |  | project-root |
| `package.json` | config |  | project-root |
| `src/analyze/acknowledge.ts` | source | service | remote-integration |
| `src/analyze/analyze-shared.ts` | source | utility | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/consolidation-guard.ts` | source | service | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/decompose.ts` | source | service | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/dedupe.ts` | source | utility | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/diff.ts` | source | utility | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/extract.ts` | source | service | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/file-validation.ts` | source | utility | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/guided.ts` | source | service | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/index.ts` | source | entrypoint | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/llm-bridge.ts` | source | utility | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/modify-reason.ts` | source | service | prd-analysis-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/propose.ts` | source | types | prd-analysis-pipeline |

| `tsconfig.json` | config |  | project-root |
| `vitest.config.ts` | config |  | project-root |

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
